---
title: "sammy_r21 — scRNA-seq QC report"
subtitle: "Pre-filter QC across the cohort, to choose clustering thresholds"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    fig-width: 11
    fig-align: center
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
# | tags: [setup]
from pathlib import Path

import anndata as ad
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from matplotlib.lines import Line2D
from scipy.stats import median_abs_deviation

# Every h5ad is staged into this notebook's working directory by the Nextflow
# process, so globbing is the whole input contract — no params to pass or drift.
H5ADS = sorted(Path(".").glob("*.h5ad"))
if not H5ADS:
    raise FileNotFoundError("No .h5ad files staged next to the notebook.")

# Condition palette, carried over from oir-analysis so figures across the two
# projects read the same. Teal/orange is a blue-orange axis, which stays separable
# under the common red-green CVDs.
COLORS = {"normal": "#2AABB8", "obese": "#E87D2A"}

METRICS = {
    "n_genes_by_counts": "Genes per cell",
    "total_counts": "UMI counts per cell",
    "pct_counts_mt": "Mitochondrial %",
    "pct_counts_in_top_20_genes": "% counts in top 20 genes",
}


def load_obs(path):
    """Read one h5ad's obs without pulling X into memory.

    backed="r" leaves the count matrix on disk; obs is all this report needs, and
    the cohort's X would be ~5 GB.
    """
    adata = ad.read_h5ad(path, backed="r")
    obs = adata.obs.copy()
    adata.file.close()
    return obs


obs = pd.concat([load_obs(p) for p in H5ADS], ignore_index=True)
# Order samples by condition then id so the panels read as two blocks, and the
# figures below can share one sample order.
obs["id_num"] = obs["id"].astype(int)
sample_order = (
    obs[["sample", "condition", "id_num"]]
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values(["condition", "id_num"])["sample"]
    .tolist()
)
obs["sample"] = pd.Categorical(obs["sample"], categories=sample_order, ordered=True)
sample_condition = obs.drop_duplicates("sample").set_index("sample")["condition"].to_dict()
```

## Cohort

```{python}
n_samples = len(sample_order)
n_cells = len(obs)
by_cond = obs.groupby("condition", observed=True)["sample"].nunique()
print(f"{n_samples} samples · {n_cells:,} cells (pre-filter)")
print(" · ".join(f"{c}: {n} samples, {(obs['condition'] == c).sum():,} cells" for c, n in by_cond.items()))
```

Every barcode Cell Ranger called is included — `create_adata` filters nothing, so
these are the raw distributions the filtering decision gets made against.

## Per-sample summary

```{python}
rows = []
for s in sample_order:
    d = obs[obs["sample"] == s]
    rows.append({
        "sample": s,
        "condition": sample_condition[s],
        "cells": len(d),
        "median genes": round(d["n_genes_by_counts"].median()),
        "median counts": round(d["total_counts"].median()),
        "median mito %": round(d["pct_counts_mt"].median(), 2),
        "cells <200 genes": int((d["n_genes_by_counts"] < 200).sum()),
        "cells >10% mito": int((d["pct_counts_mt"] > 10).sum()),
        "cells >20% mito": int((d["pct_counts_mt"] > 20).sum()),
    })
summary = pd.DataFrame(rows)
summary.style.hide(axis="index").format({"median counts": "{:,}", "median genes": "{:,}"})
```

The last three columns are what fixed thresholds would cost, for orientation only —
they are not a recommendation.

## Distributions

Each panel is one metric across all samples; colour is condition. Log scale on the
count and gene axes, since both span orders of magnitude.

```{python}
# | fig-height: 13
fig, axes = plt.subplots(len(METRICS), 1, figsize=(11, 3.2 * len(METRICS)))

for ax, (metric, label) in zip(axes, METRICS.items()):
    data = [obs.loc[obs["sample"] == s, metric].to_numpy() for s in sample_order]
    parts = ax.violinplot(data, showextrema=False, widths=0.85)
    for body, s in zip(parts["bodies"], sample_order):
        body.set_facecolor(COLORS[sample_condition[s]])
        body.set_alpha(0.75)
        body.set_edgecolor("none")
    # Median as a thin white tick — a violin hides the centre, and the centre is
    # what the threshold conversation is about.
    medians = [np.median(d) for d in data]
    ax.scatter(range(1, len(data) + 1), medians, marker="_", s=90, color="white", zorder=3, linewidths=1.4)

    if metric in ("n_genes_by_counts", "total_counts"):
        ax.set_yscale("log")
    ax.set_xticks(range(1, len(sample_order) + 1))
    ax.set_xticklabels(sample_order, rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
    # Title carries the metric name; a y-label would just repeat it.
    ax.set_title(label, loc="left", fontsize=11, pad=8)
    ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.25, linewidth=0.5)
    ax.set_axisbelow(True)

handles = [Line2D([0], [0], marker="s", linestyle="", markersize=9, markerfacecolor=c, markeredgecolor="none", label=k)
           for k, c in COLORS.items()]
axes[0].legend(handles=handles, frameon=False, loc="upper right", fontsize=9)
fig.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Per-sample QC scatter

The joint view is what separates debris from real cells: low-count/low-gene points in
the bottom-left, and dying cells riding high on mitochondrial fraction. Colour is
mitochondrial %, clipped at 25% so the ramp is not dominated by a few extreme cells.

```{python}
# | fig-height: 9
n_cols = 5
n_rows = int(np.ceil(len(sample_order) / n_cols))
fig, axes = plt.subplots(n_rows, n_cols, figsize=(11, 2.3 * n_rows), sharex=True, sharey=True)
axes = np.atleast_1d(axes).ravel()

for ax, s in zip(axes, sample_order):
    d = obs[obs["sample"] == s]
    sc = ax.scatter(
        d["total_counts"], d["n_genes_by_counts"],
        c=d["pct_counts_mt"].clip(upper=25),
        cmap="viridis", vmin=0, vmax=25,
        s=1.5, alpha=0.35, edgecolors="none", rasterized=True,
    )
    ax.set_xscale("log")
    ax.set_yscale("log")
    ax.set_title(f"{s}\n{len(d):,} cells", fontsize=8, color=COLORS[sample_condition[s]])
    ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)
    ax.tick_params(labelsize=7)

for ax in axes[len(sample_order):]:
    ax.set_visible(False)

fig.supxlabel("Total counts", fontsize=9)
fig.supylabel("Genes detected", fontsize=9)
cbar = fig.colorbar(sc, ax=axes.tolist(), fraction=0.02, pad=0.02)
cbar.set_label("Mitochondrial % (clipped at 25)", fontsize=8)
cbar.ax.tick_params(labelsize=7)
plt.show()
```

## Suggested per-sample thresholds (MAD-based)

Each sample's cutoffs derived from its own distribution — median ± N·MAD, computed on
`log1p` for the count and gene metrics (they are right-skewed, so a symmetric band only
makes sense in log space) and on the raw scale for mitochondrial %. This is the
sc-best-practices formulation: 5 MADs for counts/genes, 3 for mito.

These are a **starting point, not an answer.** Read them against the plots above and
override any sample where the data disagrees.

```{python}
def mad_bounds(values, n_mads, log=False):
    """Return (lower, upper) cutoffs at median ± n_mads · MAD, on the original scale."""
    v = np.log1p(values) if log else np.asarray(values, dtype=float)
    med = np.median(v)
    mad = median_abs_deviation(v)
    lo, hi = med - n_mads * mad, med + n_mads * mad
    return (np.expm1(lo), np.expm1(hi)) if log else (lo, hi)


rows = []
for s in sample_order:
    d = obs[obs["sample"] == s]
    genes_lo, genes_hi = mad_bounds(d["n_genes_by_counts"], 5, log=True)
    counts_lo, counts_hi = mad_bounds(d["total_counts"], 5, log=True)
    _mito_lo, mito_hi = mad_bounds(d["pct_counts_mt"], 3)

    drop = (
        (d["n_genes_by_counts"] < genes_lo) | (d["n_genes_by_counts"] > genes_hi)
        | (d["total_counts"] < counts_lo) | (d["total_counts"] > counts_hi)
        | (d["pct_counts_mt"] > mito_hi)
    )
    rows.append({
        "sample": s,
        "condition": sample_condition[s],
        "min genes": round(genes_lo),
        "max genes": round(genes_hi),
        "min counts": round(counts_lo),
        "max counts": round(counts_hi),
        "max mito %": round(mito_hi, 2),
        "would drop": int(drop.sum()),
        "% dropped": round(100 * drop.mean(), 1),
        "retained": int((~drop).sum()),
    })
suggested = pd.DataFrame(rows)
suggested.style.hide(axis="index").format({"min counts": "{:,}", "max counts": "{:,}", "min genes": "{:,}", "max genes": "{:,}"})
```

```{python}
total_drop = suggested["would drop"].sum()
total_keep = suggested["retained"].sum()
print(f"Cohort: would drop {total_drop:,} of {n_cells:,} cells ({100 * total_drop / n_cells:.1f}%), retaining {total_keep:,}.")
```
